headline: Should EOG’s 70% Cash-Return Pledge Guide Capital Allocation Decisions for EOG (EOG) Investors?
summary: In the past quarter, EOG Resources reported adjusted Q1 2026 earnings of US$3.41 per share, supported by higher production, strong cash flow, and a reiterated plan for steady 2026 capital spending while reallocating more capital toward liquids. An interesting angle for investors is EOG’s pledge to return at least 70% of annual net cash flow after capital expenditures to shareholders through dividends and buybacks, underscoring its focus on disciplined cash distribution over pure production...
